Okay, so I am actually very excited for this presentation. I was kind of hesitant to make it, but I felt that it was really necessary and I want to talk about building emotional intelligence. So the reason I want to talk about this is because I really believe that in order to be a great leader, then you have to be very self-aware. And if you're not able to manage your state of mind, then how can you help others manage their state of mind? Right? It goes for your team, it goes for customers, et cetera. So I want to talk about the skills that you need to be emotionally healthy. And why should you care about this? Again, in order to manage your mental state and the mental state of others, it has to start with mastering it yourself. And so the best thing that I can do for everyone here is to make you aware of what it means to be emotionally healthy and hopefully bring to light some of the things that you're doing right now that might not be. And remembering that people do not leave jobs, they leave bosses. And that starts with you. And if you are not the best version of yourself for your business and for your team, then there is a chance that they will leave you. And so end of the day, it comes down to being the best person that you can be while on this earth. And I think that that starts with being aware of where your flaws lie, right? And so remembering also, you will not attract fulfilled, motivated, emotionally stable people if you're not that. And so if you want those kind of people in your business, you must become that kind of person first and you must become a better version of that than anybody else, right? So starting with what is emotional health? Okay, there are five key components that I want to talk about. Okay, and I say all of this and I'm talking about all out of experience because before we ever started this business, I took a while to work on my emotional health because I didn't feel it was where I should be. And I didn't know what I wanted to do, but what I did know is I wanted to be a better person and I wanted to have more control over my mental state. Okay, and I feel as though I've gotten to a point where I have in leadership because of that. And so it starts with this is your foundation that you must lay so that you can build your business on top of it, right? So number one component is understanding your emotions. Okay, what does it mean to understand the motion? It means that you are able to name it, you're able to feel it and you're able to identify it. Okay, and so the thing is that most of us use generic terms. Like when someone says, how are you feeling? You're like, I feel fine, I feel good, bad, tired, whatever, right? You don't hear people talking about, I feel inflated, euphoric, I feel frustrated, you know, angry, a little bit depressed, I feel a lot of doubt, right? Instead of people just use fine, good, bad, tired. And the thing is you have to be able to identify exactly what it is. This is something I started doing back when I was like 18. And I don't remember what book I read in, it might have been 20 Robins. And I sat in front of a mirror and every time I felt like an underlying sense of emotion, I would look at myself in the mirror and I would name it and I would say it out loud to myself in the mirror. And so I know that sounds really woo-woo, but like, heck, it works. And I still do that to this day. Okay. And the thing is in order to be able to identify what the emotion is, you have to be able to sit with it long enough to uncover it. And what most of us do is we avoid our emotions and we hide them. But in order to identify it, you have people to sit there and you have to be thinking about it like, okay, what is it that I'm feeling right now? Am I sad? Am I fearful? Am I angry? Am I sad with myself? Am I angry with myself? And so you have to be able to pull yourself out of the turbine of emotion and to be able to sit there and say like, really think through it. Like, what am I feeling right now? And why am I feeling that? Right? Second is you have to allow those emotions. Okay. What does that mean? That means you allow yourself to feel something without resistance and fear. Like being an amazing, fearless leader doesn't mean that you don't fear crap. I get scared all the time. And I'm sure all of you do too. Okay. And I let myself be scared. I'm like, okay, I'm scared as fuck or I'm super scared. Like, what is it? And I sit there and I think about my fear and I think about why I'm scared. I think about all the terrible things that could happen. And usually when I let myself have that five minutes to think about it, it kind of goes away after that because I've allowed myself to experience the emotion. If you are constantly resisting emotions, they will never go away. Okay. They will just continue to be there. It'll cycle through your brain over and over again. Okay. And your job is not to tell someone how you feel, but to let yourself feel it. Right. And so what I mean by that is that. You don't need to tell people how you feel. You need to let yourself feel it because it's not their responsibility to do anything about it. It's your responsibility to feel that emotion and to be okay with the fact that it's uncomfortable. You know what I'm saying? Why? Because emotions will not kill you. And I think a lot of people, especially nowadays with social media being like everyone has to be, you know, you have to put your persona out there and you do this and that. It's like, we can't feel emotions. We can't be humans who experience things. It's bullshit. Like we all get sad, angry, afraid, cry, whatever. Right. Um, and that the real truth of it is like, you can feel something without having an enormous reaction to it. Okay. And that's like the number one point I would like to drive home is that you can feel I'm an emotion and not do anything about it. You can just sit there and feel it. In fact, that's what you should do about it. You should sit and feel it and be uncomfortable with the fact that you feel like crap. Right. Because there are emotions and then there are choices. It is your choice to react to an emotion. He did not make you do this. The team did not make you do this. Your parents did not make you do this. It's your choice to react to an emotion. No matter what somebody else does, it doesn't matter how you choose to react is all up to you. Okay. Emotion does not equal reaction. This is like personal development. One on one. If you've ever read any book about personal responsibility, like you are responsible for your emotions. Nobody else, like the way that you react to a situation has nothing to do with the situation itself. Okay. Emotions are not tied to circumstance. What one person does is not responsible for how you feel. Okay. And when you blame others for how you feel, you give up your power to be able to change. Okay. And that's really sad because that would mean that most of your life would be the result of other people's actions rather than your own. And so remember, nobody makes you feel anything. You choose to feel it. It is up to you. This is a control you have over. And I think that traditional or conventional wisdom talks about how to manage emotions. Once you feel them, but the thing is, is that you get to choose how you feel and how we react. You know what I mean? Anyways, you have to be able to be at peace and be content knowing that there are going to be times when you will not be peaceful and content. If that makes any sense at all, like you have to be okay with the fact that life is not a constant state of happiness. It's not a constant state of peace. Life is like this. Okay. It's always like that. And so you have to accept that. So many people go about resisting it. They're like, I just want to be happy. I just want to be content. It's like, you're not freaking going to be. This is life. Like you're a human. You're not designed that way. Okay. And once you know that, it feels a lot better because you're like, I'm a human. It's okay to go up and down. Like this is normal, especially a human that owns a business. Okay. So remember, it is okay. And it is human to not be happy all the time and to experience emotions, especially negative ones, totally okay. Okay. Resisting emotions and stonewalling everyone and everything is the surest way to feel worse than the people who allow themselves to feel any emotion. Okay. If you want to live a miserable life, then try to stonewall and numb out everything else because then nobody will want to be around you. Nobody's going to be married to you. Nobody's going to want to work with you because you're just dull. Right. Because you've just numbed the entire world rather than facing it, confronting your feelings and letting yourself experience the emotions. Right. Have you ever seen those people? It's like, I see those people all the time. I'm like, that person's freaking emotionally constipated. Like they haven't felt shit in years. Okay. You don't want to be that person. That's not being alive. Number three is not indulging in emotions. Okay. Indulging in emotions is what keeps you stuck. Right. And so I really want to hammer this one home. Um, what you do is you replay the same scenario over and over and over again. It's like, he broke up with me. He cheated on me. I can't believe he did this. God, I just, I just can't believe he did this. I just can't believe this happened two days later. I just can't believe this happened. I just get what a dick. What a dick. How often do we hear that? Right. It's like the typical you see in the movies, the girl dwelling over the breakup. The guy cheating on her, et cetera. Right. Don't be that person. Um, and the thing is, is that we indulge in the familiar negative emotion because we are afraid of feeling an unfamiliar negative emotion. Right. And so I kind of want to dive into that. Um, why I think that's so important to understand and to be able to identify in yourself is because you're going to feel a negative emotion anyways. So you might as well make it a productive one. Right. Like you're going to feel like shit if you're poor and you're going to feel like shit when you're trying to make money because you're failing and failing and failing until you finally have success. So like you might as well do the one that makes you money. Right. And so another example is like you can have the negative emotion of feeling bad because you're overweight and you can just constantly be like, I look like shit today. I look fat. There's my roles. There's my muffin top, or you can have the negative emotion of being like, I feel really uncomfortable at the gym because I feel like a fat ass. I feel really uncomfortable at the gym because I don't know what I'm doing, but I'm on my way to losing weight. Right. You choose which discomfort you want to have. One has a productive outcome. One does not. Another example that would be not hitting your quota. I can see this. I see this a lot in the workplace. Like you don't do something well and instead of taking all of your energy into investing into a new way to solve the problem, you dwell on how bad it makes you feel. You dwell on the fact that you feel like, oh my God, I could get fired. I'm not good enough for this job. All the stuff. Rather than being like, shut this down. I'm going to go be uncomfortable taking massive action. Right. To change how I feel right now. Both are uncomfortable. One has a productive outcome. One does not. So both feel bad, but at least one of them is going to move you forward in life. Right. That's the way I look at it. Four is you don't hide from emotions. Okay. Avoid in situations because of the fear of the feelings that will come with it. Okay. This is huge in sales. Why do most people suck at sales? Most people suck at sales because they're afraid of what they're going to feel when someone says no. Right. They're hiding from an emotion, which is why I do believe that in order to be good at sales, you have to have a certain level of self awareness because you have to have mastered your emotional state. You have to have mastered understanding that fear is okay. It's okay to be rejected. Right. I'm not worried about getting into a sale because I don't care and I'm not afraid of how I feel if they say no. I have felt rejection before. I know what it feels like. I will feel it again after you've done it, you know, thousands of times. It doesn't really phase you. Right. And that's why confidence and that's why salespeople are so confident because confidence comes from the willingness to feel any emotion. You're like, okay, I'm going in the sale. I could actually leave feeling elated, depressed, angry, frustrated, doubtful. I'm willing to feel any of them. And so I'm going to go in there super confident. Right. And the surest way to be unhappy is to constantly try to be happy and not actually confront how you feel. So the people who go about life hiding from their emotions are the most unhappy of all, because they feel that no matter what they do, their life is just this way. It's a feeling of stuck because instead of confronting the uncomfortable emotions of change, they live in the uncomfortable emotions of same. And so they just have the same scenario, the same problems, the same everything over and over again. They live in the same shit rather than living in new shit that at least leads to a productive life. Right. And again, like you're going to feel like crap avoiding the emotions and you're going to feel like crap confronting them and not avoiding them. So you might as well take the one where you are being productive. Right. And there's a difference between being strong and being numb. Right. Numb avoiding, et cetera. You need to choose wisely when you're doing these things. Number five, creating emotion on purpose. Okay. Concept that I would really like to break down. You have to understand that only you can generate your emotions. Right. And so it's not the neighbor. It's not your employees. It's not the market. It's not Facebook. It's you that generates your emotions. Right. How many, you all know someone who you consider very wise, very mature, because it's like no matter what's happening around them, they seem very common centered. And that's because they probably feel their emotions. They don't avoid their emotions. They let themselves experience it. And then they're more likely to be more likely to be more likely to be more likely to remember that it's okay to feel that way. And that brings you a sense of peace and calm that cannot be replaced. Right. Remembering that your thoughts are what are going to dictate your emotions. Okay. And you can create the emotion you want to feel on purpose. How do you do that? You do that because of your thoughts. Right. And this doesn't mean that you should or will be happy all the time. Again, I keep throwing this in there because I think it's just this elusive lie that society puts out that like there's so many things out nowadays. The happiness product, the five second bit, like all this crap. It's like, dude, you're not going to be happy all the time. And if you aim for that goal, you're going to be very severely disappointed. You're going to feel like crap about yourself because you're not happy all the time. And the reality is you won't be happy all the time. So just accepting it and understanding that like being a human is awesome and it's cool and it's fine to have all sorts of emotions along the way. Like that's totally fine. Right. The last thing you want to do is be emotionally concentrated or in a concentrated disappointment because you are disappointing yourself. They're not happy all the time. They're ridiculous. Right. Creating emotion on purpose. Okay. This is your thoughts, create your emotions. So what happens to you isn't what creates your emotions. What happens creates thoughts and those thoughts can be dictated by you. You don't have to dwell in your negative thoughts. Right. That takes a certain level of awareness where you can sit back, think about a situation at hand and state yourself, okay, I'm having really negative thoughts right now. I'm going to choose to not think about this stuff. And I'm going to think about this. I'm going to focus on productive thoughts. How can I mend this situation? How could I look at this differently? What would someone smarter than me do? Right. And that creates better emotions than thinking yourself, I'm a freaking failure. This sucks. The whole business is going under. I'm fucked. Right. That's just a negative cycle that you're going to live in. Okay. And so if you want to generate better emotions, you need to think better thoughts. And that is something that you have control over. And it's something that a lot of people aren't taught when they're younger. It's just like, oh, you have, hey, you make me feel this way. I thought these things because you said this. And the reality is it's all up to you. Like you can feel and think whatever you want. It's just that you have to believe that before you can do it. And if you want to change who you are, ultimately you need to change the way you think. Right. And that starts with understanding what it looks like to be emotionally healthy. If you want to change who you are, then you need to change the thoughts you think. If you want to change other people, then you have to have mastered changing yourself and mastered changing the way you think before you can master changing the way other people think. Right. And just like getting strong happens from lifting day in and day out for 20, 30 years, getting mentally strong comes from intentional thinking day in and day out. Right. It comes from every day you telling yourself you're going to choose productive thoughts over destructive ones. Every day slowing down, becoming aware of what you're thinking, thinking yourself, is this what somebody wiser than me would do? Is this what someone smarter than me would do? Is this what someone more centered than me that would be thinking? Okay. So how do you go about doing this tactically? Right. What are the tactics behind thinking this way? The truth is that in order to do any of these practices, in order to achieve any of these skills, you need to first take the first step of creating the space in your life to address it. Okay. And you cannot do that unless you have time to yourself. You set time aside. You do things without the laptop around you all the time, without your phone. Right. You need to be able to spend time alone, without a computer, without a phone, where you can sit and think. Right. And so it's interesting, but like some of the best entrepreneurs and business owners of all time, one, that they're able to spend a lot of time by themselves. And two, they're not having destructive thoughts when they're doing it. Right. Because they don't avoid their emotions. Because if you're avoiding your emotions, all the thoughts in your head are just going to be destructive because you haven't resolved and let yourself feel that other issue. And then you don't want to be by yourself because the thoughts you have are shitty. Right. So if you confront your emotions, you're then able to be by yourself. You're then able to be by yourself. And then you're able to have constructive, productive thoughts that can move the ball forward. Right. You need time to be alone with your thoughts because you cannot change what you do not understand. And so if you can sit and be alone with your thoughts, you can understand where they stem from, can understand and identify beliefs that you carry that are no longer serving you. Right. And so you first must seek to understand your mind and the thoughts it habitually creates. Right. And so if you have a bad habit or if you have an action that you're repeatedly doing, instead of looking at the action and thinking, what do I need to do differently? You have to look at the thought that creates the action. What thought am I having that's creating this emotion that's leading to this action? Right. You could break it down like overeating. What am I thinking that's causing an emotion that's making me feel so overwhelmed or depressed or angry that I need to eat an entire cake? Right. It's becoming aware of where the thought stems from. So I hope this was helpful to all of you. I would watch it a couple of times. I know it's a lot of stuff, but reality is that in order to lead a team of people, in order to influence lives of others, you have to first be the master of your own self. Right. And so that starts with understanding how you think and how to control where your emotions and thoughts lie.
